currentStory.title: Show items with no store assigned in an 'Unassigned' section when grouping is Store & Category (React only)
currentStory.scope: React web app only. Swift/iOS untouched. Guest lists unaffected.
acceptanceCriteria: Items with no store appear in an 'Unassigned' top-level group when sort is Store + Category.
'Unassigned' appears after all real stores (bottom of list).
Inside 'Unassigned', items are sub-grouped by Category, mirroring real Store->Category behavior.
Missing categories within Unassigned still fall into the existing 'Other' category bucket.
Unassigned uses gray color #bbb (matches 'Other' category convention).
Behavior also applies when sort is Store-only (existing Unassigned bucket must not regress).
Checked list section also shows the same Unassigned grouping.
Verified in browser in light + dark mode.

exploreContext.bug: When Store+Category is active, unassigned-store items go through nested category grouping (subResult.groups), but current code only keeps subResult.items ?? ungrouped and discards subResult.groups. Result: items render as a flat list under bespoke 'Unassigned' header instead of as a proper top-level group with sub-groups.
exploreContext.approach: Mirror store-alone Unassigned handling, but when remaining levels produce subResult.groups, emit a top-level group object (label 'Unassigned', color '#bbb', type 'store', with subGroups) placed after sorted real stores. Compare against existing 'Other' category pattern at sortPipeline.js:341-352 and :398-405 for shape.
keyFiles: src/utils/sortPipeline.js (groupByStore L210, applySortPipeline L522, Other category L341-352 and L398-405)
src/components/ShoppingList.jsx:225-245 (current bespoke 'Unassigned' rendering for ungrouped — may need removal/adjustment)
src/utils/listTypes.js:212-231 (guest lists do not — no regression risk)
exploreContext.itemStoreField: UI uses item.store; DB column is store_id. null/undefined/empty-string all treated as unassigned.
exploreContext.tests: No existing tests for applySortPipeline or groupByStore. Vitest is the framework.

lockedDecisions: Inside 'Unassigned', sub-group by Category (mirror real store sections).
Use gray color #bbb for the 'Unassigned' top-level group.

specialistsBlock.quality: (none — falls back to commands.lint and commands.quality)

highPriority: npm run lint baseline has 28 pre-existing errors in unrelated files; quality routing cannot cleanly distinguish new regressions from baseline debt.
mediumPriority: No pre-existing tests for applySortPipeline / groupByStore (partially addressed by sortPipeline.test.js).
No regression tests for guest/RSVP list sort grouping.

handoff.whatJustHappened: Builder ran the full per-story pipeline for TSK-20260505-01 (Unassigned store group when grouping is Store + Category). Developer (react-dev) implemented the fix in src/utils/sortPipeline.js. Quality (npm run lint) clean for changed files. Tester (react-tester) created src/utils/sortPipeline.test.js with 6 new tests; npm test passes (7 total). Critic returned 0 blocking and 2 advisory findings. toolkit-critic appended project-level gaps to the session gap report.
handoff.whereWeAre: Story is at the post-story Human Review Gate. No commit yet. Two advisory findings are unresolved and awaiting the user's decision: (1) gate the new Unassigned section on nextLevel === 'category', (2) add a guest-list regression test for AC #8.
whatComesNext: User chooses one of: 'approve' (commit as-is), 'tighten' (re-dispatch Developer to tighten the gate AND Tester to add a guest-list regression test, then re-run Quality + Critic), or specify other changes.
If approve: commit the changes on main with a message referencing TSK-20260505-01.
If tighten: re-delegate fixes, re-run Quality and Critic, then return to the Human Review Gate. Max 2 quality re-runs per story.
openQuestions: Approve as-is, tighten the advisory findings, or apply different changes?
contextToPreserve: Branch: main. No commit yet for this story.
Locked decisions still in force: sub-group Unassigned by Category; use color #bbb.

Advisory finding #1 (react-critic): src/utils/sortPipeline.js:283 — Unassigned section is created for any nested grouping under Store, not strictly Store+Category. Practically unreachable today (guest lists disallow Store grouping per listTypes.js:212-231). Suggested fix: gate on nextLevel === 'category'.
Advisory finding #2 (react-tester): no test asserts guest/RSVP lists are unaffected (AC #8). Suggested: add guest-list regression test calling applySortPipeline with the guest list type.

Quality result: src/utils/sortPipeline.js lints clean. Project-wide npm run lint has 28 pre-existing errors in unrelated files (.tmp/, ai-tmp/, e2e/, playwright.config.js, src/context/*.jsx) — flagged as project-level gap, not blocking this story.
Tests: 7 passing total (6 new in sortPipeline.test.js).
Specialists: react-dev (dev), react-tester (test), react-critic (critic). Quality runs npm run lint. Security falls back to global security-critic.

Session gap report (project-level only): high — clean up lint baseline; medium — add docs/CONVENTIONS.md + conventions/testing.md + conventions/naming.md; medium — expand regression coverage for sort/grouping utils and guest/RSVP grouped lists. No global toolkit issues.
